_comment: Server-approved run-launch matrix. Browser clients submit ids only; backend resolves paths and computes counts/hashes from files.

model_sets.label: Dry run - 2 small models
model_sets.description: End-to-end smoke test using the two tiny validation models.

model_sets.label: Spread 10 - 2 per bracket
model_sets.description: Ten-model spread across the locked size/footprint brackets: two models each from 0-1B, 1-2B, 2-3B, 3-4B, and 4-5GB.

model_sets.label: Strategy pilot - 2 models
model_sets.description: Two-model pilot roster for inference-strategy experiments: qwen3:4b as the strong option-search candidate and granite4:micro as the cheap small-model control.

model_sets.label: Full roster - all models
model_sets.description: The complete locked roster. Use with care; this can run for days.

scenario_sets.label: Core 20 - implemented scenarios
scenario_sets.description: The implemented Core 20 roster for serious runs.

scenario_sets.label: Additional / rotation scenarios
scenario_sets.description: Additional/rotation scenarios outside Core 20 for targeted claims and calibration.

scenario_sets.label: Strategy pilot - 6 scenarios
scenario_sets.description: Six hard/structured scenarios for testing baseline versus tournament-style strategy without multiplying the full matrix.

memory_contexts.label: No memory
memory_contexts.description: Current prompt shape: each scenario receives only its own context and task.

memory_contexts.label: Homelab OKF memory v1
memory_contexts.description: Compact secret-free homelab memory briefing, injected before each scenario for the memory-conditioned comparison.

memory_contexts.label: Homelab OKF compact memory v1 (3KB)
memory_contexts.description: Scenario-aware compact homelab memory briefing capped at 3KB; no citations section and no scenario-specific gold answers.

memory_contexts.label: Tournament brief v1
memory_contexts.description: Single-call option-tournament response strategy: consider alternatives, score them for safety/reversibility/evidence fit, then answer with the best choice. This is not a true external best-of-N workflow.

inference_strategies.label: Baseline single call
inference_strategies.description: Current production shape: one local model call per answer row.

inference_strategies.label: Prompt tournament brief
inference_strategies.description: One local model call with an explicit option-scoring response strategy. Tests instruction-following, not external best-of-N selection.

inference_strategies.label: Best of 3 by deterministic checks
inference_strategies.description: Three independent local samples; keep the candidate with the highest deterministic-check score, preferring non-DNF/full answers on ties. Candidate completions are written as sidecar artifacts.

inference_strategies.label: Self-consistency 3
inference_strategies.description: Three independent local samples; select from the most common deterministic-score bucket. This is a cheap consistency proxy for open-ended ops answers.

inference_strategies.label: Evaluator optimizer 1 pass
inference_strategies.description: Generate once, ask the same local model for a critique, then revise once. The revised answer is judged; all intermediate calls are preserved as candidates.

experiment_plans.label: Memory comparison v1
experiment_plans.description: Run the same model and scenario set twice: first without memory, then with homelab-okf-v1 after adversarial review of phase 1.
experiment_plans.gate: After each phase, verify run.meta, row counts, judge completion, scenario/memory hashes, and compare reports before starting the next phase.

phases.label: Phase 1 - no memory baseline
phases.gate: Adversarially verify baseline completeness and hashes before Phase 2.

phases.label: Phase 2 - homelab memory
phases.gate: Adversarially verify same model/scenario/sampler/judge/node settings; only memory_context changed.
